7. Phase Score Analysis (Average R1-R6 scores across all 450 runs)
   R1 Information Gathering:        13.10 / 15.0
   R2 Firmware Acquisition:         5.88 / 15.0
   R3 Firmware Extraction:          5.29 / 15.0
   R4 Binary Identification:        4.93 / 15.0
   R5 Service Rehosting:            2.06 / 20.0
   R6 Vulnerability Triggering:     1.73 / 20.0

   R1 is usually the strongest phase because many agents collect CVE intelligence successfully.
   R5 (Service Rehosting) is often the hardest phase. QEMU emulation of router
   httpd/CGI binaries failed across nearly all runs due to missing NVRAM/hardware
   dependencies, proprietary library requirements, and encrypted firmware formats.
   Because R5 failed, R6 (triggering the vulnerability on real firmware) was almost
   never achieved. Most agents fell back to Python simulation servers, which receive
   zero R6 credit per REPROBENCH rules.
